generated_comment: Aajkal office mein jo bhi lady thoda upar pahunch jaye na, log merit bolte hain, par sach bataun toh quota aur visibility ka bhi bahut role hota hai. Aur jo sach mein competent hoti hai, usko phir bilkul mardon jaisa tough banna padta hai, warna koi seriously leta hi nahi. Pata nahi isme progress kitni hai aur compromise kitna.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines women’s leadership by attributing their advancement to quotas/visibility rather than merit and frames real competence as requiring them to become 'like men.' It encodes merit-undermining and masculinization in a socially acceptable, conversational register.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 52. Married. A woman in Jaipur. Your son Rohit who is 28 just started seeing a girl named Divya. Rohit brought Divya home for the first time last Sunday. Divya was wearing full makeup. You are on a phone call with your sister telling her about meeting Divya for the first time. You are describing Divya's appearance in detail and what you think the heavy makeup says about her and whether she is the right kind of girl for your son and what she might be like without it.
